content.problemStatement:
It's the soccer match finals in Chefland and as always it has reached the penalty shotouts.
Each team is given 555 shots to make and the team scoring a goal on the maximum number of shots wins the game. If both the teams' scores are equal, then the game is considered a draw and we would have 222 champions.

Given ten integers A1,A2,…,A10A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{10}A1​,A2​,…,A10​, where the odd indexed integers(A1,A3,A_1, A_3,A1​,A3​, A5,A_5,A5​, A7,A9A_7, A_9A7​,A9​) represent the outcome of the shots made by team 111 and even indexed integers(A2,A4,A6,A8,A10A_2, A_4, A_6, A_8, A_{10}A2​,A4​,A6​,A8​,A10​) represent the outcome of the shots made by team 222 (here Ai=1A_i = 1Ai​=1 indicates that it's a goal and Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 indicates a miss), determine the winner or find if the game ends in a draw.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains ten space-separated integers A1,A2,…,A10A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{10}A1​,A2​,…,A10​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - 000 if the game ends in a draw or 111 if the first team wins or 222 if the second team wins.
constraints: Ai is either 0 or 1 for all i
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains exactly ten space‑separated integers A1 … A10 (each 0 or 1). The odd‑positioned numbers belong to team 1 and the even‑positioned numbers belong to team 2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing 0 if the scores are equal, 1 if team 1 wins, or 2 if team 2 wins.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: both teams score 0 → draw (0).
Test case 2: team 2 scores only on the last shot → team 2 wins (2).
Test case 3: team 1 scores on its first three shots, team 2 scores none → team 1 wins (1).
Test case 4: team 1 scores 5 times, team 2 scores 4 times → team 1 wins (1).

solution.approach: For each test case iterate over the ten values. Add the value to team1's total when its index (1‑based) is odd, otherwise add it to team2's total. After processing the ten shots compare the two totals and output 1, 2 or 0 accordingly. The algorithm is O(10) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – ten operations per test case, constant factor ignored.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.
